title: Deep repository analysis and upstream synchronization
description: Analyze repository state, check all branches for unmerged work, evaluate upstream commits from LiteLLM/LLMRouter, identify technical debt and areas needing attention. Document findings for other work streams.
closeReason: Deep repository analysis complete. All TG10 branches confirmed merged, identified 4 unpushed commits, 3 pending dependabot PRs, upstream drift analysis complete (~40 commits behind), exceptional test coverage verified (1.61:1 ratio). Comprehensive spec written covering branch state, technical debt, CI/CD health, and prioritized recommendations. No blocking issues found.

title: Formalize deployment and configuration patterns
description: Create universal deployment configurations for any environment (Docker, K8s, cloud platforms). Formalize persistence options (Redis, Postgres, S3). Create environment-specific config templates. Ensure deployable anywhere.
closeReason: Comprehensive persistence and config pattern analysis complete. Spec written to .overstory/specs/RouteIQ-6ef5.md. Found: 5 Docker scenarios, production K8s chart, all persistence backends implemented (PostgreSQL/Redis/S3/GCS), 50+ env vars, AWS deployment guide. Identified 6 critical gaps: no deployment matrix, scattered env docs, missing GCP/Azure guides, no config generator, no migration guides, no environment configs. Recommended 4-week formalization plan.

title: Model integration and routing enhancement
description: Enhance model integration to support any LLM provider. Improve routing strategies and ML models. Optimize model selection algorithms. Ensure seamless model hookup for all providers.
closeReason: Comprehensive exploration of routing strategies and ML models complete. Documented: 18+ strategies, dual integration approaches (monkey-patch + CustomRoutingStrategyBase), progressive enhancement chain (ML → Centroid → Random), security model (pickle protection, hash verification), performance characteristics (Centroid ~2ms, InferenceKNN ~10-50ms), and provider integration patterns. Spec file: .overstory/specs/RouteIQ-8ab4.md

title: Production hardening and quality improvements
description: Fix jank areas in codebase. Add comprehensive testing. Improve error handling and resilience. Enhance observability and monitoring. Ensure production-grade quality throughout.
closeReason: Test coverage and observability gaps cataloged. Found: 6 untested core modules (~7k LOC), 7 untested plugins (~2265 LOC), routes lack OTel spans. Spec written with P0-P3 recommendations. Notable: strong fixture infrastructure, 3031 tests total but critical security gaps.

title: Documentation and pattern formalization
description: Document all modes and patterns. Create comprehensive deployment guides. Formalize architectural decisions. Update README and CLAUDE.md with latest patterns.
closeReason: Deployment & config patterns documented: 5 Docker modes, Helm chart, 8 config presets explored. Identified 10 doc gaps (decision matrix, ADRs, production checklist). Spec written with recommendations.

title: P0 — CDK Foundation (VPC/EKS Auto Mode/IRSA/ECR/cdk-nag)
description: Stand up deploy/cdk/ RouteIqStack porting VSR substrate constructs: network_construct (VPC+7 SGs+11 endpoints incl BEDROCK_RUNTIME+S3 gw), eks_cluster_construct (L1 CfnCluster Auto Mode + IRSA-via-CfnJson factory + OIDC), ecr_construct (immutable+scan + GHCR pull-through cache for ghcr.io/baladithyab/routeiq), nag_suppressions (AwsSolutionsChecks). Parameterize for RouteIQ single stateless pod + chart IRSA SA annotation (values.yaml:206). One pod role: aps:RemoteWrite + rds-db:connect + elasticache:Connect + AppConfig read + Bedrock invoke. Deliverable: cdk/ tree + CDK unit+snapshot tests (cred-free pytest synth+nag gate). ADRs 0030,0027. ~3-4w. OPERATOR-GATED deploy.
closeReason: P0 CDK Foundation COMPLETE (commits 2dbac45 proposal + 3a90096 build). deploy/cdk/ RouteIqStack: VPC+endpoints, EKS Auto Mode L1 + Pod Identity (not IRSA), GHCR pull-through + governance, cdk-nag, single least-privilege pod role. Cred-free gate VERIFIED: uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/ = 36 passed (no AWS creds). Chart seams wired + helm lint passing. All 7 child tasks closed. ADR-0030 amended (full rewrite tracked as RouteIQ-1f1b, low pri). Deploy remains operator-gated.

title: P1 — Externalized State (Aurora Serverless v2 + ElastiCache Serverless Valkey)
description: Port replay_store_construct (Aurora PG Serverless v2: scale-to-zero, IAM auth, KMS, 30d rotation, schema-bootstrap Lambda+CR — pin enum AND in-region version) + cache_construct (ElastiCache Serverless Valkey: IAM-auth user group user_id==user_name, KMS, always-on TLS REDIS_SSL=true). Wire chart externalPostgresql/externalRedis (values.yaml:316-338) + ESO ClusterSecretStore + boot-render DATABASE_URL. Deploy Aurora in SEPARATE CI stage (~30min rollback). ADRs 0028,0029. ~2-3w.

title: P2 — Config + Observability + Data Lake (AppConfig + AMP/AMG/CW + Firehose)
description: Port config_state_construct (AppConfig App/Env/Profile/Strategy/Version/Deployment + LAMBDA validator + S3 + SNS) + observability_construct (AMP CfnWorkspace + AMG flag-gated + CW dashboard + 9 alarms + 3 metric filters + 2 log groups w/PII DataProtectionPolicy) + data_lake_construct (routing_decision CW Logs->Firehose JSON->Parquet->S3->Glue partition-projection->Athena). Wire AppConfig poll adapter + OTel gen_ai.* emit + structured routing_decision log line. CW log group MUST be CDK-created (MetricFilter pre-exist). ADRs 0026,0027. ~3w. Overlaps P1.

title: P3 — Routing + MLOps (Kumaraswamy-Thompson + adapter framework + closed loop)
description: NET-NEW (RouteIQ differentiation): Kumaraswamy-Thompson bandit as CustomRoutingStrategyBase plugin (src/litellm_llmrouter/kumaraswamy_thompson.py, posteriors->Aurora, hot EMA->ElastiCache, arm-config->AppConfig), registered via get_routing_registry().register(). Pluggable RoutingAdapter framework + MLOps loop (telemetry->train->sign->hot-reload). Wire eval_pipeline.py to data-lake corpus. De-risk against preserved corpus (241 rows/237 SFT tuples) OFFLINE first. docs 20,40. ~4-6w.

title: P4 — Governance / Multi-Tenant / UI Hardening on AWS substrate
description: WIRE governance.py + usage_policies.py -> Aurora (durable multi-tenant rows); rate-limit windows -> ElastiCache. oidc.py + React UI behind ALB/ACM + ESO client-secret. Optional: native Bedrock Guardrail (CfnGuardrail flag-gated) + WAF (waf_construct). HARDEN networkPolicy (egress Aurora:5432/cache:6379) + readOnlyRootFS probe. ADRs 0020,0008. ~3-4w.

title: P0 CDK: port network_construct.py (VPC + SGs + endpoints)
description:
Port deploy/cdk/lib/network_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws/cdk/lib/network_construct.py, adapted for RouteIQ's single stateless pod. VPC routeiq:vpc_cidr default 10.40.0.0/16 (distinct from VSR 10.20/10.30), max_azs=2, nat_gateways=1 (keyword-only, default floor), enable_dns_hostnames + enable_dns_support ON. Three subnet tiers: public (PUBLIC), private-app (PRIVATE_WITH_EGRESS — pod ENIs + interface endpoints), private-data (PRIVATE_ISOLATED — reserved for P1). Tag public kubernetes.io/role/elb=1, private kubernetes.io/role/internal-elb=1. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/network_construct.py exists exposing self.vpc, self.public_subnets, self.private_app_subnets, self.private_data_subnets, the 3 SGs (alb_sg/pod_sg/vpce_sg), self.interface_endpoints (6 entries incl BedrockRuntime), and self.s3_gateway_endpoint. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: network_construct.py — VPC 10.40/16, 3 subnet tiers, 3 SGs (two-phase), 5 interface endpoints + S3 gw, flow logs. Verified: 6 VPCEndpoint resources synthesized.

title: P0 CDK: port eks_cluster_construct.py with EKS Pod Identity (NOT IRSA)
description:
Port deploy/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws, but BAKE IN THE POD IDENTITY DECISION: use EKS Pod Identity, NOT IRSA, per the research verdict (research/notes/final_report_eks-automode-cdk-irsa-podidentity-9dc4a2.md, very high confidence; AWS names Pod Identity recommended for Auto Mode; AWS docs state the eks-pod-identity-agent is built into Auto Mode, BUT the production VSR Auto Mode CDK installs eks-pod-identity-agent explicitly via eks.CfnAddon (vllm-sr-on-aws/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py, comment: 'must be present for Pod Identity associations to resolve') — resolve. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/eks_cluster_construct.py emits an AWS::EKS::Cluster with the 3 Auto-Mode blocks enabled (compute/storage/elastic-load-balancing), bootstrap_self_managed_addons=False, authentication_mode=API. The pod_identity_association() helper emits AWS::EKS::PodIdentityAssociation with a static pods.eks.amazonaws.com trust. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: eks_cluster_construct.py — L1 CfnCluster K8s 1.33 + 3 Auto-Mode blocks; EKS Pod Identity (CfnPodIdentityAssociation) NOT IRSA + defensive pod-identity-agent CfnAddon. Verified: 0 OIDCProvider, 0 CfnJson, 2 PodIdentityAssociation, 2 Addon.

title: P0 CDK: port ecr_construct.py (GHCR pull-through cache + RepositoryCreationTemplate governance on the cached repo)
description:
Port deploy/cdk/lib/ecr_construct.py from vllm-sr-on-aws, parameterized DOWN from VSR's 7 repos to RouteIQ's single gateway repo + the GHCR pull-through cache rule. GOVERNANCE TARGET CORRECTION: the immutable-tag + scan-on-push payoff applies to the repo the PTC rule AUTO-CREATES on first pull (<acct>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/ghcr/baladithyab/routeiq), NOT a standalone ecr.Repository (nobody pulls from a standalone repo, so governance on it is unobserved; ECR's default for an un-templated PTC repo is tag-MUTABLE, no scan). […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/ecr_construct.py emits one AWS::ECR::PullThroughCacheRule with UpstreamRegistryUrl=ghcr.io, UpstreamRegistry=github-container-registry, EcrRepositoryPrefix=ghcr, and CredentialArn taken from the construct param (never a literal secret); one AWS::ECR::RepositoryCreationTemplate with AppliedFor=['PULL_THROUGH_CACHE'], Prefix='ghcr', ImageTagMutability='IMMUTABLE' (DependsOn the PTC rule); and a registry scan-on-push for ghcr/* via AWS::ECR::RegistryScanningConfiguration (ScanFrequen. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: ecr_construct.py — GHCR CfnPullThroughCacheRule + CfnRepositoryCreationTemplate(IMMUTABLE) + registry SCAN_ON_PUSH; NO standalone repo; credential_arn is a param. Verified via synth.

title: P0 CDK: RouteIqStack wiring + app.py + cdk.json routeiq: context keys (ONE stack)

Build the composition root deploy/cdk/lib/routeiq_stack.py + deploy/cdk/app.py + deploy/cdk/cdk.json + requirements.txt + README.md. ONE stack for P0 (single stateless pod, one VPC/cluster/ECR/pod-role) — Aurora is P1 and gets a SEPARATE stack/CI-stage per the ~30-min-rollback rule, so the split does NOT happen at P0. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/ tree exists with app.py (the 3 helpers + AwsSolutionsChecks aspect added before synth), cdk.json (app=python3 app.py + routeiq:* context defaults incl vpc_cidr=10.40.0.0/16 and sa_namespace/sa_name), requirements.txt with exactly the 4 pins (no ecs-codedeploy), and lib/routeiq_stack.py wiring NetworkConstruct + EksClusterConstruct + EcrConstruct + the one pod role + the pod_identity_association + the 6 CfnOutputs + self._suppress_nag() last. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: routeiq_stack.py + app.py + cdk.json (routeiq: keys) + requirements + root pyproject cdk extra. ONE stack; pod role Bedrock+Secrets+S3+Logs, no AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity; 6 CfnOutputs; _suppress_nag last. 57 resources synth.

title: P0 chart: add loadBalancerSourceRanges + Pod Identity SA binding + AWS_REGION + EcrGhcrPrefix image override
description:
Close the chart seams the CDK foundation requires (deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/). GAP-1 (genuine chart gap): under EKS Auto Mode managed load balancing the NLB CIDR allowlist is honored via spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges, NOT the legacy service.beta.kubernetes.io/aws-load-balancer-internal annotation. Add (1) service.loadBalancerSourceRanges: [] to values.yaml and (2) render spec.loadBalancerSourceRanges in templates/service.yaml when type==LoadBalancer (guard like the existing nodePort block). Without this an Auto-Mode LoadBalancer Service is open to 0.0.0.0/0. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: P0 EDGE DECISION: loadBalancerSourceRanges ships INERT-UNTIL-LoadBalancer and the public edge is explicitly DEFERRED — values.yaml stays service.type: ClusterIP (verified default at deploy/charts/routeiq-gateway/values.yaml:69) and ingress.enabled: false (:82), so the Auto-Mode managed LB does NOT render by default and alb_sg + loadBalancerSourceRanges have no live consumer at P0; the public edge attaches only when an operator later flips service.type to LoadBalancer. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: chart seams — service.yaml loadBalancerSourceRanges (verified inert at ClusterIP, emits allowlist at LoadBalancer via helm template); values.yaml AWS_REGION seam; Pod Identity SA binding (no role-arn annotation). Bonus: fixed pre-existing grafana-dashboard Helm token collision.

title: P0 CDK: cdk-nag evidenced suppressions (nag_suppressions.py)
description:
Build deploy/cdk/lib/nag_suppressions.py copying only the SHAPE of the VSR file (NOT its 2400 lines). apply_nag_suppressions(stack) fans out to _suppress_<construct>(stack) helpers; each calls NagSuppressions.add_resource_suppressions_by_path(stack, '/<stack_id>/<Construct>/<Resource>/Resource', [NagPackSuppression(id, reason)]). Flag-gated families guarded with getattr(stack, 'x', None) because add_*_by_path RAISES on an absent path under cdk-nag>=2.27. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: deploy/cdk/lib/nag_suppressions.py exposes apply_nag_suppressions(stack) + per-construct helpers with getattr guards. Every NagPackSuppression has a non-empty id, an explicit appliesTo, and a reason containing a why-safe clause, a least-privilege/only-valid-form clause, and an Owner: line. After self._suppress_nag(), uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/unit/test_cdk_nag.py reports zero surviving AwsSolutions-* errors and zero CdkNagValidationFailure at both the default and maximal flag surface.

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: nag_suppressions.py — apply_nag_suppressions + per-construct getattr-guarded helpers, evidenced AwsSolutions reasons. test_cdk_nag green (0 surviving AwsSolutions errors at default + max flags).

title: P0 CDK: cred-free pytest synth + nag + snapshot test suite
description:
Build deploy/cdk/tests/ (unit + snapshot) running fully offline. The gate command is `uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/` — every test calls Template.from_stack(stack) with an explicit dummy env=cdk.Environment(account='123456789012', region='us-west-2'); no AWS creds, no cdk CLI, no network. Wire into the pre-push hook / CI. […see proposal §refs]

ACCEPTANCE: `uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/` passes with zero AWS creds and no network. […see proposal §refs]

Parent epic: RouteIQ-5a85 (P0). Full spec: docs/architecture/aws-rearchitecture/31-p0-cdk-foundation-proposal.md
closeReason: DONE 3a90096: cred-free pytest suite — 36 passed (synth+nag+snapshot+no-irsa guard), VERIFIED by my own 'uv run pytest deploy/cdk/tests/' run with no AWS creds/network. Snapshot baseline dev.json committed.

title: ADR-0030: full rewrite/rename beyond the amendment (IRSA->PodID in title; refresh L1-vs-L2 rationale)
description: The 2026-06-14 amendment header flips IRSA->Pod Identity but the filename 0030-eks-auto-mode-irsa-substrate.md and body still say IRSA. Also the amendment notes (acceptedIssue) that aws-cdk-lib/aws-eks-v2 now makes AUTOMODE the default L2 over native L1, so the 'L1 forces this' framing is a CHOICE inherited from VSR, not a hard constraint. When P0 eks construct lands, do a proper ADR rewrite: rename or supersede the file, refresh the L1-vs-L2 rationale (not just IRSA-vs-PodID). Low priority — the amendment header is sufficient for now.

title: P1 forward-notes: STS endpoint returns for rds-db IAM token; ElastiCache fail-open spend window
description: Carry-forward notes from the P0 review for the P1 state wave: (1) STS interface endpoint was dropped from P0 (correct — Pod Identity creds come from the agent, not STS web-identity) but RETURNS at P1 because rds-db IAM-auth token issuance needs it (discover-network:176-177). (2) governance+quota are fail-open on Redis loss, so ElastiCache Serverless scale-to-zero cold starts open a spend/rate window unless *_FAIL_MODE=closed — ride this on the P1 elasticache:Connect grant. (3) the migrate initContainer is P1-gated (deployment.yaml:42-44 gates on externalPostgresql.host), inert at P0. Fold into P1 epic RouteIQ-6078 planning.
